name: Apple-Cheese Soup
recipeIngredient: 8 tart apples, peeled, cored, and quartered (Granny Smith)
4 sl bacon (apple- or hickory-smoked), for garnish
1 green apple, for garnish (as Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized saucepan, heat the peanut oil over medium-high heat.
2. Add ham scraps (if using), celery, garlic, and onions; sauté for about 4 minutes, or until onions are translucent but not brown.
3. Reduce heat to medium; add apples. Cook, stirring frequently, for about 10 minutes, or until apples soften.
4. Stir in port; simmer for 5 more minutes.
5. Add chicken broth, bouquet garni, and ham bone (if using). Simmer low, partially covered, for about 20 minutes, or until flavors are well blended. Remove bouquet garni.
6. Meanwhile, fry bacon over medium heat for about 5 minutes, or until browned and crisp. Drain on paper towels; cut into 1-inch dice. Set aside.
7. Cut green and red apples into 1/8-inch dice to use for garnish. You will need about 2 tablespoons of each color. Put diced apples in a small glass or ceramic bowl and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon lemon juice. Set aside.
8. In a small bowl, combine softened butter and flour; work until smooth (beurre manie). Whisk into soup to thicken it for 5 minutes.
9. Add grated cheese to the soup, stirring constantly, until melted.
10. Strain soup through fine sieve into top of double boiler set over gently boiling water to keep hot. Do not press on solids. Season with remaining lemon juice and salt; Tabasco sauce to taste.
11. Ladle soup into warm serving bowls; garnish with diced apples and chopped bacon. Serve hot.
(Note: The soup may be made up to 2 hours in advance, refrigerated, then completed at serving time.)
